flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of the iterative linked list reversal algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(n) time complexity with O(1) additional space

flashcards.front: Name the algorithm that uses fast and slow pointers to detect cycles.
flashcards.back: Floyd's cycle-finding algorithm (also called tortoise and hare algorithm)

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of implementing stacks/queues with linked lists vs arrays?
flashcards.back: Dynamic sizing - no fixed capacity limitations and efficient memory usage

flashcards.front: How do you find the middle element of a linked list efficiently?
flashcards.back: Using the two-pointer technique: slow pointer moves one step, fast pointer moves two steps per iteration

flashcards.front: What is concatenation of linked lists?
flashcards.back: The operation of appending one linked list to the end of another linked list
